Getting Started

Configuration

Menu Configuration

Component Types

Actions

Animations

Placeholders

Reference

Commands & Permissions

On this page

Actions ​

Actions are strings that run top-to-bottom. They can be used inside a button's actions list, and in the menu-level on-open and on-close lists.

Available actions ​

ActionExampleDescription
[close][close]Closes the menu
[player] <cmd>[player] spawnRuns a command as the player
[console] <cmd>[console] give %player% diamond 1Runs a command from console
[message] <text>[message] <green>Hello!Sends a message to the player
[broadcast] <text>[broadcast] <gold>%player% joined!Broadcasts to all players
[openmenu] <id>[openmenu] user_profileCloses current menu and opens another
[sound] <key>, <vol>, <pitch>[sound] entity.player.levelup, 1.0, 1.2Plays a sound to the player

Example ​

yaml
my_button:
  type: BUTTON
  text: "<aqua>Go to Spawn"
  x: 0.0
  y: 0.0
  size: 1.5
  actions:
    - "[close]"
    - "[player] spawn"
    - "[sound] entity.player.levelup, 1.0, 1.0"
    - "[message] <green>Teleported!"

Notes ​

  • %player% is always replaced with the player's name in any action string.
  • All other PlaceholderAPI placeholders are also parsed in action strings.
  • [openmenu] closes the current menu before opening the new one.
